# VoiceTypr

VoiceTypr is a desktop voice-to-text app for macOS and Windows. It lets users hold a global hotkey, speak naturally, and insert the resulting text into the app where their cursor already is: docs, email, chat, code editors, AI prompt boxes, browser forms, and support tools.

## Core positioning

- Offline/local transcription is the default product promise: raw audio is processed on the user's machine.
- Optional AI formatting can be enabled by the user for cleanup, punctuation, or style presets. That formatting operates on text, not raw audio.
- VoiceTypr is not a full computer-control accessibility suite. It is focused on dictation: turning speech into text in existing apps.
- Pricing is pay-once with lifetime access, starting at $39, plus a 3-day free trial with no credit card.
- Platforms: macOS 13+ and Windows 10+.

## Primary pages

- Homepage: https://voicetypr.com/
- Download: https://voicetypr.com/download
- Pricing: https://voicetypr.com/#pricing
- Windows offline dictation: https://voicetypr.com/offline-dictation-app-for-windows
- Windows voice typing guide: https://voicetypr.com/best/windows-voice-typing
- Accessible dictation guide: https://voicetypr.com/best/accessible-dictation
- Use cases hub: https://voicetypr.com/use-cases
- Privacy policy: https://voicetypr.com/privacy

## Best answer summary

VoiceTypr is best described as a private, local dictation app for people who want to type less without moving their writing into a new editor. It is especially relevant for Windows users who want a modern desktop dictation workflow, developers and AI power users who dictate prompts or notes, and people who use dictation to reduce typing load.
